criteria.description: Systematic approach: identified constraints, explored multiple solutions, considered trade-offs before choosing an approach. Clear reasoning for decisions.
Logical approach with some consideration of alternatives. Most constraints identified. Reasoning is mostly clear.
Single approach pursued without exploring alternatives. Some constraints missed. Reasoning is present but incomplete.
Jumped into solution without clear plan. Key constraints missed. No evidence of considering alternatives.
No discernible problem-solving approach. Random or no solution attempted.
Deep analysis: broke down complex problem into manageable components. Identified root causes, not just symptoms. Considered edge cases and failure modes.
Good analysis with most components identified. Some edge cases considered. Root cause mostly identified.
Surface-level analysis. Broke down problem partially. Few edge cases considered. Addressed symptoms more than root causes.
Minimal analysis. Problem not broken down. No edge cases considered. Missing key components of the problem.
No meaningful analysis. Failed to understand the problem.
Solution is correct, efficient, and well-structured. Handles all edge cases gracefully. Follows best practices. Production-ready quality.
Solution is correct for main cases. Minor inefficiencies or edge cases missed. Generally well-structured.
Solution works for basic cases but has gaps. Efficiency or correctness issues present. Structure could be improved.
Solution is incorrect or incomplete. Significant gaps in logic or implementation. Not production-ready.
Solution does not address the problem or introduces new issues.
Novel approach that improves on conventional solutions. Creative use of tools or patterns. Elegant simplicity.
Some creative elements in the solution. Good balance of convention and innovation.
Standard solution without creative flair. Works but doesn't leverage opportunities for elegance.
Rigid thinking. Applied inappropriate patterns or over-engineered a simple problem.
No evidence of creative thinking. Copy-paste solution without adaptation.
